title: Capacity, Observability, Support, and Production Operations · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for capacity, observability, support, and production operations contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in operational readiness and capacity case and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.
Measure task availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, and support from the user boundary and correlate them with system signals.
Design logs, metrics, traces, profiles, audits, retention, access, redaction, sampling, cardinality, and cost around diagnostic questions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Capacity Model
steps.instruction:
debug case — Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.

Inspect the Capacity Model trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Capacity Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Capacity Model, keep Capacity Model connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Capacity Model, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Capacity Model, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Capacity Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Capacity Model, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.
For Inspect the failure record · Debug Capacity Model, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A successful load test reveals production capacity.

stimulus.title: Capacity Model case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A successful load test reveals production capacity.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty. Artifact under review: operational readiness and capacity case.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Capacity Model, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Capacity Model record says operational readiness and capacity case looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Capacity Model.

For inspect the failure record, apply Capacity Model without its stated preconditions, omit the operational readiness and capacity case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Capacity Model for operational readiness and capacity case.

stimulus.title: Capacity Model failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A successful load test reveals production capacity.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.

options.text: Another successful Capacity Model run of the same operational readiness and capacity case happy path. That claim fails the capacity model · fault isolation evidence boundary for Capacity Model.

For capacity model · fault isolation, apply Capacity Model without its stated preconditions, omit the operational readiness and capacity case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Capacity Model for operational readiness and capacity case.
A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Capacity Model.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Game Day
steps.instruction:
Append a capstone-incident-game-day-vma6v3: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Game Day.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Incident Game Day into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Incident Game Day, keep Incident Game Day connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
capstone-incident-game-day-vma6v3:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Incident Game Day structural check is only one gate. operational readiness and capacity case must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Incident Game Day
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Incident Game Day: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Incident Game Day.
steps.why: Retrieving Incident Game Day through the concrete operational readiness and capacity case case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Incident Game Day, keep Incident Game Day connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Incident Game Day, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Restoring the happy path completes an incident exercise.
